summary: Phase 3 Add-Location Optimization backend regression: 18/18 tests PASSED. Verified (A) `client_quality` is stripped from public_spot_view for non-admin viewers on both GET /api/spots/{id} and GET /api/feed/home, while admins still receive `{score, signals, captured_at}`; (B) GET /api/admin/pending honours ?sort=quality|newest|oldest with deterministic ordering and falls back to quality on unknown values (?sort=garbage); (C) Phase 2 regression intact — POST /api/spots without new fields, POST with data_quality_signals=null, GET /api/spots/{id} on a public spot all return 200, and free-tier saves cap (3 → 4th=402) and collections cap (0 → 1st=402) still gate via 402. Test file uses fresh isolated test users via /api/auth/register and direct-Mongo seed of visibility_status='pending_review' to bypass admin auto-approve. All TEST_ data cleaned up at end.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/admin.py admin_pending: the `quality` sort uses two consecutive list.sort() calls relying on Python's stable sort — correct, but a single key=(quality_desc, ts_desc) tuple sort would be slightly clearer. Behaviourally fine.

server.py public_spot_view: `client_quality` strip happens BEFORE the `privacy_mode == 'private'` early-return that yields None. Net behaviour is correct (None never carries client_quality) but the pop is wasted work for private spots. Micro-optimization only — not a bug.

Admin role tuple in public_spot_view (admin, super_admin, moderator) matches the GET /admin/pending guard (require_role('moderator')) — `support` role would see client_quality via admin/pending but NOT through public_spot_view (since 'support' is not in the admin tuple). Verify intent: support has read access on the pending queue dashboard but client_quality is hidden from them. Likely intentional, worth confirming with PM.
seed_data_creation: All TEST_ spots and TEST users created during the run are deleted at the end of the suite (DELETE /api/spots/{id} + direct-Mongo purge fallback for pending_review spots; direct-Mongo delete for users + their spot_saves + collections).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Re-running test_phase2_add_location.py back-to-back with this file from the same IP can trip a per-IP 429 on POST /api/spots (admin: 'Too many requests. Try again in 3592s'). Either: (a) run only one of the two suites per hour, or (b) bump the rate-limit window, or (c) skip the cross-suite execution. The 429 is NOT caused by Phase 3 code — it's an unrelated rate-limit on the shared /api/spots endpoint. Test user emails use `@lumascouttest.com` (not `.local` — pydantic EmailStr rejects reserved TLDs).
rca_of_issue: n/a — no defects found. Implementation matches the spec: public_spot_view strips client_quality for any viewer whose role is not in {admin, super_admin, moderator}; the strip is also visible through home_feed because every spot in that feed flows through public_spot_view. admin/pending sort param uses stable list sort to compose (quality DESC, created_at DESC) for the default case and the alternative `newest` / `oldest` paths route to created_at sort with the expected direction. Unknown sort values fall through the else branch to the quality-sorted default.
